title: triticum spelta subsp. dicoccum
intro: Triticum spelta subsp. dicoccum, also known as emmer wheat, is an ancient subspecies of wheat that is characterized by its distinctive spikelets and hulled grains. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to help alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Emmer wheat has been found to have various health benefits, including improving cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of chronic diseases, and providing essential nutrients such as fiber, iron, and B vitamins. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and supplements, as well as being incorporated into traditional breads and baked goods.
